subject: Geography
title: Satellites and GPS
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Satellites and GPS.

hook.text: GPS uses 31 satellites orbiting Earth to pinpoint your location within two meters.

buildup.text: The US military built GPS in the 1970s. It was opened to civilians after a plane was shot down.

discovery.text: Your phone receives signals from at least four satellites and calculates position by timing.

twist.text: Without Einstein's relativity corrections, GPS would drift 10 km per day.

climax.text: GPS now guides planes, ships, tractors, and delivery drones with centimeter precision.

punchline.text: Einstein's theory keeps your map app working.

quiz.question: Why does GPS need Einstein's relativity corrections?
options: Without them, positions would drift 10 km per day
Satellites would fall out of orbit
